XVIII · Menses Amoris
18
Months of Us
Baikoo • Chai • Kitticat • Radha
Eighteen months of stolen glances, laughter in corridors, fingers intertwined, and hearts that grew bigger every single day. This is our story — made just for you.

Dukani Baideo
"Every single photo of you is a poem. A painting. A whole universe that I get to keep."
Chapter II
Our Moments
Emoji Filter Day · Under the Tree
The Most Us Photo Ever
Hearts, sunflowers, teddy bears, a rainbow — every emoji was already there and the camera still could not contain how much love was in this one frame. You pouting, me smiling like an idiot, the trees behind us doing their best to frame two people who were simply too happy to care about anything else. This is my favourite kind of us. Not dressed up. Not posed. Just completely, chaotically, joyfully ourselves.
I look at this photo and I feel the exact afternoon it was taken. The warmth. The sound of leaves. That feeling of having nowhere else to be and nobody else you want to be with. That is what you give me, Baikoo. A feeling of arriving somewhere. Of being exactly where I am supposed to be.
The Lake Moment · Don Bosco Campus
Two Hearts, One Gesture
There we are — finger hearts aimed at the camera and the whole world, two people so unbothered by everything around them. That lake behind us, those red umbrellas, the grey sky — none of it mattered. What mattered was you looking at me like that, that tiny mini-heart you made with your fingers and the smile that came right after.
I remember that afternoon like a favourite song I never get tired of. Everything about being around you feels like that — ordinary moments turned golden, every single time.
Domino's Date · The Road
Pizza and You — Best Combo
Domino's boxes in hand, greenery all around, and your head resting on my shoulder like that is exactly where it belongs. Because it does. There is no more perfect picture of us than this one — two people who do not need a fancy restaurant or a special occasion. Give us pizza, an open road, and each other, and we will make it the best day of the week.
I love that we are this kind of couple. The kind that makes ordinary places feel like destinations. Chai, you make every little outing feel like an adventure. Every moment with you is worth remembering.
The Pout · Domino's Picnic
When She Pouts, The World Stops
You are pouting at the camera and holding a Domino's box like a trophy, and I am grinning because I know exactly what you are doing. You always pout when you are pretending to be annoyed but actually could not be happier. I know all your faces, Radha. Every single one of them. The serious face. The laughing face. The almost-asleep face. The “I am fake pouting but I love you” face. This one is my favourite.
Eighteen months of collecting your faces. Eighteen months of memorising your moods. And I am still not tired. You are an endless study in wonderful.
School Days · Don Bosco College
Where It All Began
White shirts, blue lanyards, and you standing beside me under that tree with the biggest smile. This is where we started — between classes, in corridors, under trees that somehow became ours. Chai, do you remember the first time you sat next to me? I was nervous. And then you just laughed. And I was done for.
College gave us textbooks and timetables, but it also gave us each other. Every boring lecture was a little less boring with you around. Not because of the food. Because of you.
Roadside · Yellow Scarf Day
Wherever the Road Takes Us
You in your red dress, me in my yellow scarf, both of us squinting against the afternoon sun on an empty road lined with trees. Kitticat, some of our best moments have happened on roads exactly like this one. Roads that are not taking us anywhere important — just giving us an excuse to walk side by side a little longer.
We do not need a destination. We just need to be going somewhere together. Any road becomes the right road when you are beside me. Any ordinary day becomes a story worth telling.
Evening Out · The Park
Golden Hour, Golden Us
That golden evening light catching your face, the garden so perfectly still. You were wearing that maroon dress and the necklace you always wear, and I remember thinking: nobody else in the world is as beautiful as this person right here.
This photo has a kind of ease. A comfort. The way two people look when they have been in love long enough to stop performing it and just live it. That is us. That is what 18 months looks like.
Festival Day · Both in Tika
Matching Energy, Always
Both of us with tika on our foreheads, both smiling like we just got blessed — because we did. There is something deeply beautiful about celebrating festivals together. You let me into your world, Ashu. Into your festivals, your food, your family, your ordinary mornings. That is not a small thing. That is everything.
The Pinky Promise
These Hands, That Promise
A lunchbox between us, your red thread bracelet around your wrist, your fingers reaching toward mine. There is something about your hands that has always made me feel safe. Like holding your hand is coming home. Kitticat, every time I hold your hand I silently promise: I am not going anywhere.
Cultural Outfit · Golden Hour
A Queen in the Evening Light
You sitting on the grass in full traditional attire — the red embroidered fabric, the gold jewellery at your forehead, the large earrings catching the last of the evening light. You looked like you belonged in a painting. Like someone had arranged the whole world just to frame you correctly. You carry culture like you were born wearing it. Effortless. Regal. Completely, breathtakingly you.
The Family Photo
She Let Me In
When someone lets you into their family, it means something. Radha, the day you introduced me to the people who raised you, I felt something shift in my chest — a kind of warmth, a kind of belonging. You did not just give me your love. You gave me a family to belong to. And I carry that with me every single day.
I Love You · The Bouquet Moment
She Said It Without Words
A bouquet made entirely of your own face. “I love you” written right there on it. And the look on your face — that quiet, real, slightly shy smile — says everything a thousand love letters could not. Devi, you are the most beautifully silly, most genuinely loving person I know. I keep everything you send me. You are my archive of joy. My everything, saved carefully, held tightly, never forgotten.
Chapter III
A Letter to You
ALL THE NAMES I CALL YOU
Baikoo ♡
Chai ☕
Dukani Baideo
Kitticat 🐱
Baby ♡
Ashu
Love
Radha 🌸
Devi
Life ♡
My Everything
Jaan ♡
Princess
Sunshine 🌞
Each name a different facet of you.
Each name a different way I love you.
Written after 18 months of loving you
My dearest Baikoo,
I do not know exactly when it happened — the moment you stopped being just someone I knew and became the person I needed. It was not one big moment. It was a thousand tiny ones. The way you laugh with your whole face. The way you make everything feel lighter just by being there. The way you pout when you are annoyed and try to pretend you are not. The way you send me photos at night with your hair undone and still look like the most beautiful person on earth.
Chai, you are the warmth I reach for every morning. Before phone, before breakfast — there is a part of me that reaches toward you. That is not something I planned. That is just what 18 months of you does to a person. You become their default comfort. Their first thought. Their last thought before sleep.
Do you remember the days in college when we would sit outside under the trees and just talk? About everything and nothing? Those hours felt different. Time moved differently when you were near. The world was the same world but somehow everything was more alive. More colourful. More worth showing up to.
Kitticat, you have this way of looking at me that makes me feel seen in a way I have never felt before. Not just seen — understood. Like you look at me and you know the whole story behind my eyes without me having to say a single word. That kind of knowing is rare. That kind of knowing is a gift.
Radha, my Devi, my Life — I have spent 18 months collecting moments of you. Every single version of you is a version I have fallen a little bit more in love with. And on this day I want to tell you clearly: You are the best thing that has happened to me. Not just recently. Ever. And I would choose you again. In every timeline where we might have met. I would always, always choose you.
I love you, Ashu. Baby. Chai. Baikoo. Radha. Life. I love every single name you have, because every name is a different shade of my favourite person.
Forever yours ♡
— Your Boy
Chapter IV
18 Reasons I Love You
01
Your laugh — the way it starts small and then takes over your whole face and everything else in the room.
02
The way you make the most ordinary moments feel like scenes from a movie I want to watch forever.
03
How you pout when you are upset but cannot stay upset for long — the most endearing thing I have ever seen.
04
Your fearlessness — the way you wear a flower in your hair in school uniform like it is the most natural thing in the world.
05
The way you look in red. Every single time. It stops me completely.
06
How you can be soft and strong at the same time — gentle when needed, fierce when it counts.
07
The way you look at a camera — that half-aware, half-dreamy look that makes every photo feel like a painting.
08
Your sense of humour — the funny memes, the weird edits, the things that make me laugh when I least expect it.
09
How you carry the people you love — the way you care shows up in everything, quietly and constantly.
10
Your bindi. Your necklace. Your bangles. The little details of you that I have memorised and would recognise anywhere.
11
The way you exist in spaces — whether at a campus, cafe, or park — you always belong, always glow.
12
How being around you makes everything feel possible. You are the most quietly encouraging person I know.
13
Your voice when you are happy versus when you are tired — I know every version and I love them all equally.
14
The way you showed up for me — not just in big moments, but in small ones, on random days, without being asked.
15
Your birthday energy — candles, fairy lights, champagne glasses — you deserve every celebration in the world.
16
The way you have grown — more yourself, more certain, more radiant — it has been my honour to witness.
17
You chose me. Out of everyone. You chose to give your time, your laughter, your softness — to me.
18
Because after 18 months, I love you more than I did at Month 1 — and that, more than anything, is the reason.
Just for Fun

Chapter V
18 Months in Gold
Month 1
The Hello That Changed Everything
Before we even realised it was beginning, it had already begun. A conversation. A laugh. The universe quietly saying: this one.
Month 2
Learning the Little Things
I started learning you. The way you take your chai. The music you hum without knowing. I was studying you like my favourite subject.
Month 3
Oh. It is You.
Somewhere in Month 3 I stopped pretending it was casual. I knew. Oh. It is her. It is always going to be her.
Month 4
2AM Conversations
The nights we stayed up just talking. About dreams, about fears, about nothing at all. Those conversations built something that no time can take away.
Month 5
Our Places, Our Rituals
The tree we always sat under. The canteen we always visited. We made a whole map of ours out of a college campus.
Month 6
Six Months Deep
Six months. And I loved you more than I did on day one. This thing between us was real.
Month 7-8
We Stayed
Every relationship has its difficult seasons. We had ours. And we chose, every time, to stay. To work through it. To not give up on this.
Month 9
Trust as a Foundation
By Month 9, something had settled. A trust. Not blind faith but earned certainty — the kind that comes only when you have been through something together.
Month 10
Dressed in Tradition
Festivals. College events. You in a saree that made me lose my ability to form sentences. Some months are marked not by dates but by images.
Month 11
Exploring Together
Parks and cafes and every new place felt like discovering the world for the first time. Not because the places were new — but because you were seeing them with me.
Month 12 ♡
A Whole Year of You
Happy first anniversary, Baikoo. A year of choosing each other. A year of growing. What a beautiful, wild, precious year.
Month 13-15
Candles and Champagne
Your birthday. The fairy lights, the candles, the way you held that glass and closed your eyes — I wanted to freeze that moment forever. You deserve every celebration, every single year.
Month 16-17
The Quiet Kind of Loving
Not just grand gestures but Tuesday mornings. Random texts. The memes. The mirror selfies. The everyday love — constant and warm and ours.
Month 18 ♡
Here. With You.
18 months. 548 days. Countless memories. One truth: I would start all over again tomorrow if it meant falling in love with you again.
Chapter VI
Happy Birthday, Baby
"She was born — and the world got better."
This birthday setup you walked into — the banner, the candles, the fairy lights, the champagne glasses with their red velvet bows — you deserve every bit of it. Every candle flame. Every light. Every petal. You walked into that room and lit up in a way that outdid all of it.
The way you looked — eyes closed, glass in hand, the whole setup glowing around you — it looked like a scene from a dream. Because you are a dream, Ashu. Not just on your birthday. Every day. But especially on your birthday, when you let yourself be celebrated the way you always should be.
You turned another year older that night. More beautiful. More certain. More you. And I got to be there. I got to be the one who put up those lights and watched your face when you walked in. Every birthday after this, may your life be full of warmth like those candles — steady, glowing, impossible to ignore. Happy birthday, my Radha. My Devi. My everything.
XII SCIA · Classroom
The Girl Who Owned Every Room
XII SCIA on those glasses, paint-stained fingers making peace signs, school uniform with a handprint in the middle of it — this is you fully, completely, unapologetically yourself. Goofy and bold and brilliant. I love every version of you.
Chapter VII · The Ongoing One
To Infinity
18 and Counting
My Baikoo. My Chai. My Radha. My Life.
My everything with a hundred names.
This file — these pages, these words, these photos — is just a small attempt to hold in one place everything I feel for you. The truth is, no file can contain it. What I feel for you is bigger than what language was built to carry.
Here is to all the months that came before this one — all 18 of them, golden and real and ours. And here is to every month that comes after. Every morning. Every difficult Tuesday. Every unexpected joy. Every ordinary miracle that becomes extraordinary just because you are in it.
Baikoo ♡Chai ☕Kitticat 🐱 Radha 🌸Devi •Ashu ♡ Baby •Life ♡
♥
I love you yesterday, today, and every
tomorrow that has not been named yet.
— Your Boy, Month 18
XVIII · MENSES AMORIS
